- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# One-time setup of the local Supabase Postgres container for coverage runs.
# - Ensures `plpgsql_check` is preloaded (already is in current Supabase
#   images, but we still install the extension and set the profiler GUC).
# - Resets the profiler so coverage starts from a clean state.
# - Persists `plpgsql_check.profiler = on` globally so every session
#   Playwright opens contributes to coverage.
# - Writes a sentinel file (coverage/.pg-ready) consumed by collect.sh.
#
# Idempotent. Re-run after `supabase stop --no-backup` (which discards
# the container).

set -euo pipefail

CONTAINER="${SUPABASE_DB_CONTAINER:-supabase_db_pawtograder-platform}"
ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.." && pwd)"

if ! docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -qx "$CONTAINER"; then
  echo "[setup-pg] $CONTAINER is not running — start Supabase first (\`npx supabase start\`)" >&2
  exit 1
fi

# --- Ensure plpgsql_check is preloaded -----------------------------------
existing=$(docker exec -i "$CONTAINER" psql -U postgres -d postgres -tA \
  -c "SHOW shared_preload_libraries;" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

if echo "$existing" | grep -q "plpgsql_check"; then
  echo "[setup-pg] plpgsql_check already in shared_preload_libraries"
  needs_restart=false
else
  new_value="${existing:+$existing,}plpgsql_check"
  echo "[setup-pg] setting shared_preload_libraries = '$new_value'"
  # Try ALTER SYSTEM first; some Supabase images run postgres as a
  # non-superuser, in which case we fall back to editing
  # postgresql.auto.conf directly via docker exec.
  if docker exec -i "$CONTAINER" psql -U postgres -d postgres \
    -c "ALTER SYSTEM SET shared_preload_libraries = '$new_value';" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    needs_restart=true
  else
    echo "[setup-pg] ALTER SYSTEM denied — falling back to direct postgresql.auto.conf write"
    docker exec -i "$CONTAINER" bash -c \
      "echo \"shared_preload_libraries = '$new_value'\" >> /var/lib/postgresql/data/postgresql.auto.conf"
    needs_restart=true
  fi
fi

# --- Enable profiler GUC globally ----------------------------------------
# plpgsql_check.profiler is PGC_SUSET in some versions; ALTER SYSTEM fails
# for non-superusers. Detect that and write the auto.conf file directly.
echo "[setup-pg] setting plpgsql_check.profiler = on"
if docker exec -i "$CONTAINER" psql -U postgres -d postgres \
  -c "ALTER SYSTEM SET plpgsql_check.profiler = on;" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  # ALTER SYSTEM only writes postgresql.auto.conf — the GUC does not take
  # effect for new sessions until the config is reloaded (or the server
  # restarts). plpgsql_check is already preloaded in current Supabase
  # images, so the shared_preload_libraries block above leaves
  # needs_restart=false; without an explicit reload here the profiler would
  # stay off and we'd silently collect zero Postgres coverage. SIGHUP is
  # enough for this GUC, so reload rather than force a full restart.
  docker exec -i "$CONTAINER" psql -U postgres -d postgres \
    -c "SELECT pg_reload_conf();" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
else
  echo "[setup-pg] ALTER SYSTEM denied — appending to postgresql.auto.conf directly"
  # Remove any pre-existing entry to keep idempotency clean, then append.
  docker exec -i "$CONTAINER" bash -c "
    sed -i '/^plpgsql_check\\.profiler/d' /var/lib/postgresql/data/postgresql.auto.conf 2>/dev/null || true
    echo \"plpgsql_check.profiler = 'on'\" >> /var/lib/postgresql/data/postgresql.auto.conf
  "
  needs_restart=true
fi

# --- Restart if any config changed ---------------------------------------
if [[ "${needs_restart:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
  echo "[setup-pg] restarting $CONTAINER"
  docker restart "$CONTAINER" >/dev/null
  ready=false
  for _ in $(seq 1 30); do
    if docker exec -i "$CONTAINER" pg_isready -U postgres >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      ready=true
      break
    fi
    sleep 1
  done
  if [[ "$ready" != "true" ]]; then
    echo "[setup-pg] ERROR: $CONTAINER did not become ready within 30s after restart" >&2
    docker logs --tail 50 "$CONTAINER" >&2 || true
    exit 1
  fi

  # pg_isready only tells us Postgres is accepting connections. The
  # Supabase stack (auth, kong, realtime, storage) all depend on the
  # DB and need a few seconds to reconnect after a DB bounce. The next
  # workflow step (`npx supabase status -o env`) fails fast if any of
  # those still show as starting, so wait for the whole stack here.
  echo "[setup-pg] waiting for full Supabase stack to recover"
  cd "$ROOT"
  stack_ready=false
  for _ in $(seq 1 60); do
    if npx supabase status >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      stack_ready=true
      break
    fi
    sleep 2
  done
  if [[ "$stack_ready" != "true" ]]; then
    echo "[setup-pg] WARNING: supabase status still not healthy after 120s; continuing anyway" >&2
    npx supabase status 2>&1 | head -20 >&2 || true
  fi
fi

# --- Create extension + reset profiler -----------------------------------
# Functions are unqualified-callable because plpgsql_check installs into
# `public` (in Supabase images today) and `public` is in the default
# search_path.
docker exec -i "$CONTAINER" psql -U postgres -d postgres -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1 <<'SQL'
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS plpgsql_check;
SELECT plpgsql_profiler_reset_all();
SHOW plpgsql_check.profiler;
SQL

# --- Sentinel for collect.sh ---------------------------------------------
mkdir -p "$ROOT/coverage"
date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ > "$ROOT/coverage/.pg-ready"

echo "[setup-pg] done — profiler is ON, plpgsql_check is preloaded"

245	      - name: Pre-cache edge function deps (Deno)

248	        # 47 functions' deps when the 60s health-check window expired, so the
249	        # step failed before Playwright ever ran. `deno cache` is not under a
250	        # timer, so it can take as long as it needs; the bootstrap then starts
251	        # from a warm cache and becomes ready in seconds.

253	          deno cache --no-check --import-map=supabase/functions/_coverage/deno.json \

256	      - name: Start edge function coverage bootstrap

258	          set -euo pipefail

260	          # (which needs to flush V8 coverage on exit).

262	          echo $! > /tmp/edge.pid

265	          # but a cold runner importing 47 modules can still be slow.
266	          for _ in $(seq 1 120); do
267	            if curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:9998/__health__ >/dev/null 2>&1; then ready=1; break; fi

269	          done
270	          if [[ "$ready" -ne 1 ]]; then
271	            echo "Edge function bootstrap failed to become ready on :9998" >&2
272	            tail -c 50000 /tmp/edge.log || true
273	            exit 1
274	          fi
275	          echo "Registered functions:"
276	          curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:9998/__functions__ | head -c 2000
277	          echo
278	
279	      - name: Run Playwright
280	        # Chromium only: we only install --with-deps chromium, and V8
281	        # client coverage is Chromium-only anyway. Without --project
282	        # the webkit project's tests get scheduled and all fail with
283	        # "did not run" because the webkit binary is missing.
284	        #
285	        # continue-on-error: this coverage workflow is v1 informational
286	        # only and is upstream of the actual E2E gate
287	        # (Standalone E2E Tests workflow). We don't want pre-existing
288	        # E2E flakes to block coverage uploads — the failures still
289	        # show in the step output (yellow ⚠️). Once the E2E suite is
290	        # de-flaked we can flip this back on.
291	        #
292	        # ARGOS_TOKEN is intentionally NOT passed here. The Argos reporter
293	        # in playwright.config.ts uploads screenshots whenever the token is
294	        # set. This coverage run is COVERAGE=1 (minification disabled,
295	        # different rendering), chromium-only, and a subset of the suite —
296	        # uploading it to Argos under the same commit SHA clobbers the real
297	        # visual baseline produced by the deploy.yml E2E run (shows up as
298	        # mass "removed"/"changed" screenshots). Visual diffing is owned by
299	        # deploy.yml; this workflow only collects coverage.
300	        run: npx playwright test --project=chromium
301	        continue-on-error: true
302	
303	      - name: Free disk space (pre-stop)
304	        if: always()
305	        # Multi-GB of test-results/, traces, html-report. Once
306	        # Playwright has exited (or errored) we no longer need any
307	        # of it for coverage — the lcov files all live under
308	        # coverage/. Running this BEFORE the Stop step matters
309	        # because Stop's signal-handling shell can't even start if
310	        # the disk is already full.
311	        run: |
312	          du -sh test-results/ playwright-report/ .next/cache/ coverage/ /tmp/ 2>/dev/null || true
313	          rm -rf test-results playwright-report .next/cache /tmp/playwright* 2>/dev/null || true
314	          df -h "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE" || true
315	
316	      - name: Sanity check source-tree integrity
317	        if: always()
318	        # One specific file (results/page.tsx) gets enriched to only
319	        # ~half its expected line count in CI but not locally. Print
320	        # `git status` + a directed wc on that file to figure out if
321	        # anything in the workflow corrupted it between checkout and
322	        # collect.
323	        run: |
324	          echo "=== git status --short ==="
325	          git status --short | head -50 || true
326	          echo
327	          for f in "app/course/[course_id]/assignments/[assignment_id]/submissions/[submissions_id]/results/page.tsx"; do
328	            if [[ -f "$f" ]]; then
329	              echo "=== $f ==="
330	              wc -l "$f"
331	              echo "  size bytes:"; wc -c "$f"
332	              echo "  md5 disk:   $(md5sum "$f" | cut -d' ' -f1)"
333	              echo "  md5 HEAD:   $(git show HEAD:"$f" | md5sum | cut -d' ' -f1)"
334	            fi
335	          done
336	
337	      - name: Stop Next.js so V8 coverage flushes
338	        if: always()
339	        run: |
340	          # Step 1: SIGUSR2 to the Next.js process group — that
341	          # tells our instrumentation.ts to take precise V8 coverage
342	          # (which CAN see Server Component vm-loaded bundles) and
343	          # write coverage/server-cdp.json. Unlike SIGINT, this does
344	          # not terminate the process.
345	          if [[ -f /tmp/next.pid ]]; then
346	            pid=$(cat /tmp/next.pid)
347	            pgid=$(ps -o pgid= -p "$pid" 2>/dev/null | tr -d ' ' || true)
348	            if [[ -n "$pgid" ]]; then
349	              kill -USR2 -- "-$pgid" 2>/dev/null || true
350	            fi
351	            # Also signal the Next worker children directly in case
352	            # SIGUSR2 didn't propagate via the group.
353	            pkill -USR2 -f "next-server" || true
354	            # Wait for the dump file to appear, up to 30s. instrumentation.ts
355	            # writes a PID-suffixed file (server-cdp-<pid>.json), so glob for
356	            # the family rather than the bare name (which never appears).
357	            for _ in $(seq 1 30); do
358	              if compgen -G 'coverage/server-cdp*.json' >/dev/null; then break; fi
359	              sleep 1
360	            done
361	          fi
362	          # Step 2: SIGINT for clean exit so coverage/edge/* (the
363	          # deno --coverage output) gets flushed.
364	          for pidfile in /tmp/next.pid /tmp/edge.pid; do
365	            if [[ -f "$pidfile" ]]; then
366	              pid=$(cat "$pidfile")
367	              pgid=$(ps -o pgid= -p "$pid" 2>/dev/null | tr -d ' ' || true)
368	              if [[ -n "$pgid" ]]; then
369	                kill -INT -- "-$pgid" 2>/dev/null || true
370	              fi
371	            fi
372	          done
373	          # Give Deno time to flush coverage/edge.
374	          sleep 5
375	          # Belt-and-suspenders: pkill any stragglers that didn't see
376	          # the group signal.
377	          pkill -INT -f "supabase/functions/_coverage/serve.ts" || true
378	          pkill -INT -f "next-server" || true
379	          sleep 3
380	          echo "Coverage artifacts after stop:"
381	          ls -la coverage/server-cdp*.json 2>/dev/null || true
382	          # Free disk before artifact upload — Playwright leaves
383	          # multi-GB of test-results/ and playwright-report/ that we
384	          # don't need once Playwright has exited.
385	          du -sh test-results/ playwright-report/ .next/cache/ 2>/dev/null || true
386	          rm -rf test-results playwright-report .next/cache 2>/dev/null || true
387	          df -h "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE" || true
388	
389	      - name: Collect per-layer coverage
390	        if: always()
391	        env:
392	          # The client converter loads 261 V8 dumps + their source +
393	          # source maps; default 4 GB heap OOMs near the end. Bump
394	          # to 8 GB to give monocart room.
395	          NODE_OPTIONS: --max-old-space-size=8000
396	        run: npm run coverage:collect
397	
398	      - name: Upload coverage artifact (for debugging)
399	        if: always()
400	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
401	        with:
402	          name: coverage-lcov
403	          path: |
404	            coverage/*.lcov
405	            coverage/jest/lcov.info
406	            coverage/postgres.log
407	            coverage/server-cdp*.json
408	            coverage/build-cdp*.json
409	            coverage/enrich-*.log
410	          if-no-files-found: warn
411	          retention-days: 7
412	
413	      - name: Upload to Codecov (next-server)
414	        if: always()
415	        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v5
416	        with:
417	          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}
418	          files: coverage/server.lcov
419	          flags: next-server
420	          fail_ci_if_error: false
421	          disable_search: true
422	
423	      - name: Upload to Codecov (next-client)
424	        if: always()
425	        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v5
426	        with:
427	          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}
428	          files: coverage/client.lcov
429	          flags: next-client
430	          fail_ci_if_error: false
431	          disable_search: true
432	
433	      - name: Upload to Codecov (edge-functions)
434	        if: always()
435	        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v5
436	        with:
437	          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}
438	          files: coverage/edge.lcov
439	          flags: edge-functions
440	          fail_ci_if_error: false
441	          disable_search: true
442	
443	      - name: Upload to Codecov (postgres)
444	        if: always()
445	        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v5
446	        with:
447	          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}
448	          files: coverage/postgres.lcov
449	          flags: postgres
450	          fail_ci_if_error: false
451	          disable_search: true
452	
453	      - name: Capture logs on failure
454	        if: failure()
455	        run: |
456	          echo "===== next.log ====="; tail -c 50000 /tmp/next.log || true
457	          echo "===== edge.log ====="; tail -c 50000 /tmp/edge.log || true
458	          docker logs --tail 200 supabase_db_pawtograder-platform 2>&1 || true

file.content:
name: Coverage

# Root permissions are read-only. Each job opts into pull-requests:write
# only as needed (codecov-action posts the comment from the action's own
# upload; the token does the write, not the workflow GITHUB_TOKEN — but
# leaving the principle of least privilege at the workflow root).
permissions:
  contents: read

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - staging
      - main
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - "**"

# Cancel in-flight runs when a PR is pushed again.
concurrency:
  group: coverage-${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  jest:
    name: Jest coverage
    runs-on: pawtograder-ci
    timeout-minutes: 20
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write
    env:
      # tests/e2e/TestingUtils.ts calls createAdminClient() at module
      # load time. Several unit tests import it transitively, so without
      # these dummies the suite fails to *load*, not just fail
      # individual tests. Use throwaway values — none of the unit tests
      # actually talk to Supabase (they mock it).
      SUPABASE_URL: http://localhost:54321
      NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: http://localhost:54321
      SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: dummy-anon-key
      NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: dummy-anon-key
      SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: dummy-service-role-key
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0 # codecov needs base commit for diff
          persist-credentials: false
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22
      - name: Cache npm dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.npm
          key: npm-${{ runner.os }}-node-22-${{ hashFiles('package-lock.json') }}
          restore-keys: |
            npm-${{ runner.os }}-node-22-
      - run: npm ci
      - name: Run jest with coverage
        # The pre-existing `tests/unit/llm-hint.test.ts` fails with
        # `Request is not defined` on jsdom (documented as a known
        # issue in AGENTS.md). Skip it explicitly so the rest of the
        # suite + lcov upload runs cleanly. Other genuine failures will
        # still surface and turn this step yellow.
        run: |
          npx jest --coverage \
            --testPathIgnorePatterns='tests/unit/llm-hint\.test\.ts'
        continue-on-error: true
      - uses: codecov/codecov-action@v5
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}
          files: coverage/jest/lcov.info
          flags: jest
          fail_ci_if_error: false # v1: informational
          disable_search: true

  e2e:
    name: E2E coverage (Next server + client + edge + postgres)
    runs-on: pawtograder-ci
    timeout-minutes: 90
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write
    env:
      # Coverage activation flags read by Next, Playwright, edge bootstrap.
      COVERAGE: "1"
      # NODE_V8_COVERAGE is intentionally NOT set: we capture server
      # coverage via the Inspector path in instrumentation.ts, which
      # sees vm-loaded scripts. The legacy NODE_V8_COVERAGE dump would
      # add ~1-2 GB of per-process JSON to /home/runner/_work and the
      # runner ran out of disk during artifact upload last attempt.
      COVERAGE_FUNCTIONS_URL: http://127.0.0.1:9998
      NEXT_PUBLIC_COVERAGE_FUNCTIONS_URL: http://127.0.0.1:9998
      COVERAGE_FUNCTIONS_PORT: "9998"
      # Match the prod-E2E-mode pattern from AGENTS.md.
      NEXT_PUBLIC_PAWTOGRADER_WEB_URL: http://localhost:3001
      BASE_URL: http://localhost:3001
      PORT: "3001"
      # E2E test bypass for edge functions.
      E2E_ENABLE: "true"
      END_TO_END_SECRET: not-a-secret
      EDGE_FUNCTION_SECRET: some-secret-value
      GITHUB_APP_ID: "1"
      ENABLE_SIGNUPS: "true"
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          persist-credentials: false

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 22

      - uses: denoland/setup-deno@v2
        with:
          deno-version: v2.x # stock Deno 2.x; matches local dev

      - name: Cache npm dependencies
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ~/.npm
          key: npm-${{ runner.os }}-node-22-${{ hashFiles('package-lock.json') }}
          restore-keys: |
            npm-${{ runner.os }}-node-22-

      - run: npm ci

      - name: Install Playwright browsers
        run: npx playwright install --with-deps chromium

      # AGENTS.md: must NOT restore from backup — start fresh, then deal
      # with the binary-submission-files migration that can't run via
      # supabase start, then create today's audit partition.
      - name: Start Supabase (fresh)
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          # Move the storage-owner-blocked migration aside.
          mv supabase/migrations/20260217000000_binary_submission_files.sql /tmp/binmig.sql
          npx supabase start --ignore-health-check
          # Apply as superuser.
          docker exec -i supabase_db_pawtograder-platform psql -U postgres -d postgres < /tmp/binmig.sql
          docker exec -i supabase_db_pawtograder-platform psql -U postgres -d postgres \
            -c "INSERT INTO supabase_migrations.schema_migrations (version, name) VALUES ('20260217000000', 'binary_submission_files') ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;"
          mv /tmp/binmig.sql supabase/migrations/20260217000000_binary_submission_files.sql
          # Create today's audit partition.
          docker exec -i supabase_db_pawtograder-platform psql -U postgres -d postgres \
            -c "SELECT public.audit_maintain_partitions();"

      - name: Set up plpgsql_check + profiler
        run: npm run coverage:setup-pg

      - name: Resolve Supabase env (status → SUPABASE_* / NEXT_PUBLIC_*)
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          # `supabase status -o env` emits API_URL=, ANON_KEY=,
          # SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=, DB_URL= etc. Translate to the names
          # Next.js + our code expect (SUPABASE_URL,
          # NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY,
          # NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY,
          # SUPABASE_DB_URL).
          npx supabase status -o env 2>/dev/null > /tmp/supabase.env
          # shellcheck disable=SC1091
          set -a; . /tmp/supabase.env; set +a
          {
            echo "SUPABASE_URL=$API_URL"
            echo "NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=$API_URL"
            echo "SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$ANON_KEY"
            echo "NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$ANON_KEY"
            echo "SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=$SERVICE_ROLE_KEY"
            echo "SUPABASE_DB_URL=$DB_URL"
          } >> "$GITHUB_ENV"

      - name: Write .env.local
        env:
          # These are needed inside the heredoc below; they are already
          # in $GITHUB_ENV from the previous step.
          SUPABASE_URL: ${{ env.SUPABASE_URL }}
          SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: ${{ env.SUPABASE_ANON_KEY }}
          SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY: ${{ env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          # .env.local is read by Next.js (during build + at runtime)
          # and by Deno --env-file= for the edge bootstrap. Keep it
          # in sync with $GITHUB_ENV.
          {
            echo "SUPABASE_URL=$SUPABASE_URL"
            echo "NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=$SUPABASE_URL"
            echo "SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$SUPABASE_ANON_KEY"
            echo "NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=$SUPABASE_ANON_KEY"
            echo "SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=$SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY"
            echo "NEXT_PUBLIC_PAWTOGRADER_WEB_URL=http://localhost:3001"
            echo "E2E_ENABLE=true"
            echo "END_TO_END_SECRET=not-a-secret"
            echo "REDACTED"
            echo "GITHUB_APP_ID=1"
            echo "ENABLE_SIGNUPS=true"
            echo "NEXT_PUBLIC_COVERAGE_FUNCTIONS_URL=http://127.0.0.1:9998"
          } > .env.local
          # GitHub App private key: GitHubWrapper.ts initializes octokit
          # at module load time, which requires a valid-shaped RSA PEM
          # (E2E_ENABLE bypasses the actual GitHub calls). Multi-line
          # quoted form: Deno's --env-file and Next.js dotenv both
          # support it; the \n-escaped single-line form is passed to
          # octokit literally and rejected as malformed PEM.
          PRIVKEY=$(openssl genrsa 2048 2>/dev/null)
          {
            echo "GITHUB_PRIVATE_KEY_STRING=\"$PRIVKEY\""
          } >> .env.local

      - name: Build Next.js (with sourcemaps for client coverage)
        env:
          # We don't disable Sentry sourcemaps — V8 client coverage needs
          # them to resolve compiled chunks back to .ts/.tsx files.
          NEXT_DISABLE_SENTRY: "1"
        # build:coverage bakes the build-cdp Profiler hook into NODE_OPTIONS
        # *inside* the npm script. (Setting NODE_OPTIONS as step env here does
        # NOT work: `npm run build` runs `cross-env NODE_OPTIONS=...`, which
        # overwrites the inherited value and drops the --require.) The hook
        # captures genuinely-static routes that Next prerenders at build time
        # and serves from cache at runtime — those never execute under the
        # runtime Inspector. Dynamic routes (most of the app) render at request
        # time and are covered by the runtime path instead.
        run: npm run build:coverage

      - name: Start Next.js
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mkdir -p coverage/edge coverage/client
          # setsid puts the process in its own process group so we can
          # SIGINT the whole group (npm → next → workers) cleanly.
          setsid npm run start > /tmp/next.log 2>&1 < /dev/null &
          echo $! > /tmp/next.pid
          ready=0
          for _ in $(seq 1 60); do
            if curl -fsS http://localhost:3001/ >/dev/null 2>&1; then ready=1; break; fi
            sleep 1
          done
          if [[ "$ready" -ne 1 ]]; then
            echo "Next.js failed to become ready on :3001" >&2
            tail -c 50000 /tmp/next.log || true
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Pre-cache edge function deps (Deno)
        # Download every function's deps up front, OUTSIDE the readiness
        # window below. On a cold runner the bootstrap was still downloading
        # 47 functions' deps when the 60s health-check window expired, so the
        # step failed before Playwright ever ran. `deno cache` is not under a
        # timer, so it can take as long as it needs; the bootstrap then starts
        # from a warm cache and becomes ready in seconds.
        run: |
          deno cache --no-check --import-map=supabase/functions/_coverage/deno.json \
            supabase/functions/*/index.ts || true

      - name: Start edge function coverage bootstrap
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          # setsid → own process group so SIGINT propagates to deno
          # (which needs to flush V8 coverage on exit).
          setsid npm run coverage:edge > /tmp/edge.log 2>&1 < /dev/null &
          echo $! > /tmp/edge.pid
          ready=0
          # 120s: deps are pre-cached above, so startup is normally seconds,
          # but a cold runner importing 47 modules can still be slow.
          for _ in $(seq 1 120); do
            if curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:9998/__health__ >/dev/null 2>&1; then ready=1; break; fi
            sleep 1
          done
          if [[ "$ready" -ne 1 ]]; then
            echo "Edge function bootstrap failed to become ready on :9998" >&2
            tail -c 50000 /tmp/edge.log || true
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "Registered functions:"
          curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:9998/__functions__ | head -c 2000
          echo

      - name: Run Playwright
        # Chromium only: we only install --with-deps chromium, and V8
        # client coverage is Chromium-only anyway. Without --project
        # the webkit project's tests get scheduled and all fail with
        # "did not run" because the webkit binary is missing.
        #
        # continue-on-error: this coverage workflow is v1 informational
        # only and is upstream of the actual E2E gate
        # (Standalone E2E Tests workflow). We don't want pre-existing
        # E2E flakes to block coverage uploads — the failures still
        # show in the step output (yellow ⚠️). Once the E2E suite is
        # de-flaked we can flip this back on.
        #
        # ARGOS_TOKEN is intentionally NOT passed here. The Argos reporter
        # in playwright.config.ts uploads screenshots whenever the token is
        # set. This coverage run is COVERAGE=1 (minification disabled,
        # different rendering), chromium-only, and a subset of the suite —
        # uploading it to Argos under the same commit SHA clobbers the real
        # visual baseline produced by the deploy.yml E2E run (shows up as
        # mass "removed"/"changed" screenshots). Visual diffing is owned by
        # deploy.yml; this workflow only collects coverage.
        run: npx playwright test --project=chromium
        continue-on-error: true

      - name: Free disk space (pre-stop)
        if: always()
        # Multi-GB of test-results/, traces, html-report. Once
        # Playwright has exited (or errored) we no longer need any
        # of it for coverage — the lcov files all live under
        # coverage/. Running this BEFORE the Stop step matters
        # because Stop's signal-handling shell can't even start if
        # the disk is already full.
        run: |
          du -sh test-results/ playwright-report/ .next/cache/ coverage/ /tmp/ 2>/dev/null || true
          rm -rf test-results playwright-report .next/cache /tmp/playwright* 2>/dev/null || true
          df -h "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE" || true

      - name: Sanity check source-tree integrity
        if: always()
        # One specific file (results/page.tsx) gets enriched to only
        # ~half its expected line count in CI but not locally. Print
        # `git status` + a directed wc on that file to figure out if
        # anything in the workflow corrupted it between checkout and
        # collect.
        run: |
          echo "=== git status --short ==="
          git status --short | head -50 || true
          echo
          for f in "app/course/[course_id]/assignments/[assignment_id]/submissions/[submissions_id]/results/page.tsx"; do
            if [[ -f "$f" ]]; then
              echo "=== $f ==="
              wc -l "$f"
              echo "  size bytes:"; wc -c "$f"
              echo "  md5 disk:   $(md5sum "$f" | cut -d' ' -f1)"
              echo "  md5 HEAD:   $(git show HEAD:"$f" | md5sum | cut -d' ' -f1)"
            fi
          done

      - name: Stop Next.js so V8 coverage flushes
        if: always()
        run: |
          # Step 1: SIGUSR2 to the Next.js process group — that
          # tells our instrumentation.ts to take precise V8 coverage
          # (which CAN see Server Component vm-loaded bundles) and
          # write coverage/server-cdp.json. Unlike SIGINT, this does
          # not terminate the process.
          if [[ -f /tmp/next.pid ]]; then
            pid=$(cat /tmp/next.pid)
            pgid=$(ps -o pgid= -p "$pid" 2>/dev/null | tr -d ' ' || true)
            if [[ -n "$pgid" ]]; then
              kill -USR2 -- "-$pgid" 2>/dev/null || true
            fi
            # Also signal the Next worker children directly in case
            # SIGUSR2 didn't propagate via the group.
            pkill -USR2 -f "next-server" || true
            # Wait for the dump file to appear, up to 30s. instrumentation.ts
            # writes a PID-suffixed file (server-cdp-<pid>.json), so glob for
            # the family rather than the bare name (which never appears).
            for _ in $(seq 1 30); do
              if compgen -G 'coverage/server-cdp*.json' >/dev/null; then break; fi
              sleep 1
            done
          fi
          # Step 2: SIGINT for clean exit so coverage/edge/* (the
          # deno --coverage output) gets flushed.
          for pidfile in /tmp/next.pid /tmp/edge.pid; do
            if [[ -f "$pidfile" ]]; then
              pid=$(cat "$pidfile")
              pgid=$(ps -o pgid= -p "$pid" 2>/dev/null | tr -d ' ' || true)
              if [[ -n "$pgid" ]]; then
                kill -INT -- "-$pgid" 2>/dev/null || true
              fi
            fi
          done
          # Give Deno time to flush coverage/edge.
          sleep 5
          # Belt-and-suspenders: pkill any stragglers that didn't see
          # the group signal.
          pkill -INT -f "supabase/functions/_coverage/serve.ts" || true
          pkill -INT -f "next-server" || true
          sleep 3
          echo "Coverage artifacts after stop:"
          ls -la coverage/server-cdp*.json 2>/dev/null || true
          # Free disk before artifact upload — Playwright leaves
          # multi-GB of test-results/ and playwright-report/ that we
          # don't need once Playwright has exited.
          du -sh test-results/ playwright-report/ .next/cache/ 2>/dev/null || true
          rm -rf test-results playwright-report .next/cache 2>/dev/null || true
          df -h "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE" || true

      - name: Collect per-layer coverage
        if: always()
        env:
          # The client converter loads 261 V8 dumps + their source +
          # source maps; default 4 GB heap OOMs near the end. Bump
          # to 8 GB to give monocart room.
          NODE_OPTIONS: --max-old-space-size=8000
        run: npm run coverage:collect

      - name: Upload coverage artifact (for debugging)
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: coverage-lcov
          path: |
            coverage/*.lcov
            coverage/jest/lcov.info
            coverage/postgres.log
            coverage/server-cdp*.json
            coverage/build-cdp*.json
            coverage/enrich-*.log
          if-no-files-found: warn
          retention-days: 7

      - name: Upload to Codecov (next-server)
        if: always()
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v5
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}
          files: coverage/server.lcov
          flags: next-server
          fail_ci_if_error: false
          disable_search: true

      - name: Upload to Codecov (next-client)
        if: always()
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v5
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}
          files: coverage/client.lcov
          flags: next-client
          fail_ci_if_error: false
          disable_search: true

      - name: Upload to Codecov (edge-functions)
        if: always()
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v5
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}
          files: coverage/edge.lcov
          flags: edge-functions
          fail_ci_if_error: false
          disable_search: true

      - name: Upload to Codecov (postgres)
        if: always()
        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v5
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.CODECOV_TOKEN }}
          files: coverage/postgres.lcov
          flags: postgres
          fail_ci_if_error: false
          disable_search: true

      - name: Capture logs on failure
        if: failure()
        run: |
          echo "===== next.log ====="; tail -c 50000 /tmp/next.log || true
          echo "===== edge.log ====="; tail -c 50000 /tmp/edge.log || true
          docker logs --tail 200 supabase_db_pawtograder-platform 2>&1 || true

=== collect.sh ===
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Per-layer coverage collection orchestrator. Run AFTER Playwright finishes.
# Produces (under coverage/):
#   - jest/lcov.info          (already written by `jest --coverage`)
#   - edge.lcov               (from deno coverage)
#   - server.lcov             (from c8 over NODE_V8_COVERAGE dir)
#   - client.lcov             (from v8-client-to-lcov.ts)
#   - postgres.lcov           (from dump-pg.ts)
#
# Each output is per-flag for Codecov; we do NOT merge them. Codecov merges
# server-side using flag-to-path mappings in codecov.yml.

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

mkdir -p coverage

# --- Edge functions (Deno) -------------------------------------------------
if [[ -d coverage/edge ]]; then
  echo "[collect] deno coverage --lcov"
  deno coverage coverage/edge --lcov --output=coverage/edge.lcov \
    --include="supabase/functions/" \
    --exclude="supabase/functions/_coverage/" \
    --exclude="supabase/functions/_shared/.*\\.d\\.ts" \
    || echo "[collect] WARN: deno coverage failed"
else
  echo "[collect] skip: no coverage/edge dir (was the bootstrap run with --coverage?)"
fi

# --- Next.js server (Node Inspector) -------------------------------------
# Two dump families, merged by the converter:
#   server-cdp*.json — runtime, written by instrumentation.ts on SIGUSR2
#                      (see the workflow teardown step). Captures vm-loaded
#                      Server Component bundles, unlike the older
#                      NODE_V8_COVERAGE approach.
#   build-cdp*.json  — build time, written by build-cdp-hook.cjs during the
#                      coverage `next build`. Captures Server Components that
#                      are prerendered at build and served from cache at
#                      runtime (so they never re-execute under instrumentation).
if compgen -G "coverage/server-cdp*.json" >/dev/null || compgen -G "coverage/build-cdp*.json" >/dev/null; then
  echo "[collect] v8-server-to-lcov (Inspector CDP: runtime + build dumps)"
  npx tsx scripts/coverage/v8-server-to-lcov.ts \
    || echo "[collect] WARN: server CDP conversion failed"
else
  echo "[collect] skip: no coverage/server-cdp*.json or build-cdp*.json"
fi

# --- Next.js client (Chromium V8) -----------------------------------------
if [[ -d coverage/client ]]; then
  echo "[collect] v8-client-to-lcov"
  npx tsx scripts/coverage/v8-client-to-lcov.ts \
    --input coverage/client \
    --output coverage/client.lcov \
    --base-url "${BASE_URL:-http://localhost:3001}" \
    || echo "[collect] WARN: client conversion failed"
else
  echo "[collect] skip: no coverage/client dir (no per-test client dumps written)"
fi

# --- Postgres (plpgsql_check) ---------------------------------------------
# Gated on the sentinel written by setup-pg.sh. If you ran the E2E
# without enabling the Postgres profiler, this would just produce an
# empty lcov; skip cleanly.
if [[ -f coverage/.pg-ready ]]; then
  echo "[collect] dump-pg"
  npx tsx scripts/coverage/dump-pg.ts > coverage/postgres.lcov 2> coverage/postgres.log \
    || { echo "[collect] WARN: postgres dump failed — see coverage/postgres.log"; cat coverage/postgres.log; }
else
  echo "[collect] skip: coverage/.pg-ready missing (run \`npm run coverage:setup-pg\` first)"
fi

# --- Enrich client + server lcovs ----------------------------------------
# V8 byte-range coverage produces sparse line-level data: JSX content,
# prop values, and other sub-expressions don't get probes, so Codecov
# shows them as "no data". enrich-lcov.ts walks each covered file and
# adds DA:N,1 for every non-blank, non-comment source line missing a
# DA entry — files with zero covered lines are left alone so we don't
# inflate coverage % on genuinely untested files.
for f in coverage/client.lcov coverage/server.lcov; do
  if [[ -s "$f" ]]; then
    # ENRICH_DEBUG=1 makes the enricher log source line counts for
    # every block, which we need to track down the one file where
    # enrichment stops short of EOF in CI (works fine locally —
    # probably a checkout-time race or partial readFile).
    ENRICH_DEBUG=1 npx tsx scripts/coverage/enrich-lcov.ts "$f" 2> "coverage/enrich-$(basename "$f" .lcov).log" \
      || echo "[collect] WARN: enrich-lcov failed on $f"
    echo "[collect] enriched $f → see coverage/enrich-$(basename "$f" .lcov).log for per-file detail"
  fi
done

echo "[collect] done"
ls -la coverage/*.lcov coverage/jest/lcov.info 2>/dev/null || true

=== collect.sh ===
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Per-layer coverage collection orchestrator. Run AFTER Playwright finishes.
# Produces (under coverage/):
#   - jest/lcov.info          (already written by `jest --coverage`)
#   - edge.lcov               (from deno coverage)
#   - server.lcov             (from c8 over NODE_V8_COVERAGE dir)
#   - client.lcov             (from v8-client-to-lcov.ts)
#   - postgres.lcov           (from dump-pg.ts)
#
# Each output is per-flag for Codecov; we do NOT merge them. Codecov merges
# server-side using flag-to-path mappings in codecov.yml.

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

mkdir -p coverage

# --- Edge functions (Deno) -------------------------------------------------
if [[ -d coverage/edge ]]; then
  echo "[collect] deno coverage --lcov"
  deno coverage coverage/edge --lcov --output=coverage/edge.lcov \
    --include="supabase/functions/" \
    --exclude="supabase/functions/_coverage/" \
    --exclude="supabase/functions/_shared/.*\\.d\\.ts" \
    || echo "[collect] WARN: deno coverage failed"
else
  echo "[collect] skip: no coverage/edge dir (was the bootstrap run with --coverage?)"
fi

# --- Next.js server (Node Inspector) -------------------------------------
# Two dump families, merged by the converter:
#   server-cdp*.json — runtime, written by instrumentation.ts on SIGUSR2
#                      (see the workflow teardown step). Captures vm-loaded
#                      Server Component bundles, unlike the older
#                      NODE_V8_COVERAGE approach.
#   build-cdp*.json  — build time, written by build-cdp-hook.cjs during the
#                      coverage `next build`. Captures Server Components that
#                      are prerendered at build and served from cache at
#                      runtime (so they never re-execute under instrumentation).
if compgen -G "coverage/server-cdp*.json" >/dev/null || compgen -G "coverage/build-cdp*.json" >/dev/null; then
  echo "[collect] v8-server-to-lcov (Inspector CDP: runtime + build dumps)"
  npx tsx scripts/coverage/v8-server-to-lcov.ts \
    || echo "[collect] WARN: server CDP conversion failed"
else
  echo "[collect] skip: no coverage/server-cdp*.json or build-cdp*.json"
fi

# --- Next.js client (Chromium V8) -----------------------------------------
if [[ -d coverage/client ]]; then
  echo "[collect] v8-client-to-lcov"
  npx tsx scripts/coverage/v8-client-to-lcov.ts \
    --input coverage/client \
    --output coverage/client.lcov \
    --base-url "${BASE_URL:-http://localhost:3001}" \
    || echo "[collect] WARN: client conversion failed"
else
  echo "[collect] skip: no coverage/client dir (no per-test client dumps written)"
fi

# --- Postgres (plpgsql_check) ---------------------------------------------
# Gated on the sentinel written by setup-pg.sh. If you ran the E2E
# without enabling the Postgres profiler, this would just produce an
# empty lcov; skip cleanly.
if [[ -f coverage/.pg-ready ]]; then
  echo "[collect] dump-pg"
  npx tsx scripts/coverage/dump-pg.ts > coverage/postgres.lcov 2> coverage/postgres.log \
    || { echo "[collect] WARN: postgres dump failed — see coverage/postgres.log"; cat coverage/postgres.log; }
else
  echo "[collect] skip: coverage/.pg-ready missing (run \`npm run coverage:setup-pg\` first)"
fi

# --- Enrich client + server lcovs ----------------------------------------
# V8 byte-range coverage produces sparse line-level data: JSX content,
# prop values, and other sub-expressions don't get probes, so Codecov
# shows them as "no data". enrich-lcov.ts walks each covered file and
# adds DA:N,1 for every non-blank, non-comment source line missing a
# DA entry — files with zero covered lines are left alone so we don't
# inflate coverage % on genuinely untested files.
for f in coverage/client.lcov coverage/server.lcov; do
  if [[ -s "$f" ]]; then
    # ENRICH_DEBUG=1 makes the enricher log source line counts for
    # every block, which we need to track down the one file where
    # enrichment stops short of EOF in CI (works fine locally —
    # probably a checkout-time race or partial readFile).
    ENRICH_DEBUG=1 npx tsx scripts/coverage/enrich-lcov.ts "$f" 2> "coverage/enrich-$(basename "$f" .lcov).log" \
      || echo "[collect] WARN: enrich-lcov failed on $f"
    echo "[collect] enriched $f → see coverage/enrich-$(basename "$f" .lcov).log for per-file detail"
  fi
done

echo "[collect] done"
ls -la coverage/*.lcov coverage/jest/lcov.info 2>/dev/null || true

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Launch the edge function coverage bootstrap under Deno with --coverage.
# Listens on $COVERAGE_FUNCTIONS_PORT (default 9998).
#
# IMPORTANT: do NOT run `supabase functions serve` concurrently with this —
# the bootstrap is the replacement for it during coverage runs.

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

mkdir -p coverage/edge

PORT="${COVERAGE_FUNCTIONS_PORT:-9998}"
ENV_FILE="${COVERAGE_ENV_FILE:-.env.local}"

if [[ ! -f "$ENV_FILE" ]]; then
  echo "[run-edge-bootstrap] env file $ENV_FILE not found; the edge bootstrap requires the same env as 'supabase functions serve'" >&2
  exit 1
fi

exec deno run \
  --allow-env --allow-net --allow-read --allow-write --allow-sys \
  --allow-import \
  --coverage=coverage/edge \
  --env-file="$ENV_FILE" \
  --import-map=supabase/functions/_coverage/deno.json \
  supabase/functions/_coverage/serve.ts

  # images, so the shared_preload_libraries block above leaves

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# One-time setup of the local Supabase Postgres container for coverage runs.
# - Ensures `plpgsql_check` is preloaded (already is in current Supabase
#   images, but we still install the extension and set the profiler GUC).
# - Resets the profiler so coverage starts from a clean state.
# - Persists `plpgsql_check.profiler = on` globally so every session
#   Playwright opens contributes to coverage.
# - Writes a sentinel file (coverage/.pg-ready) consumed by collect.sh.
#
# Idempotent. Re-run after `supabase stop --no-backup` (which discards
# the container).

set -euo pipefail

CONTAINER="${SUPABASE_DB_CONTAINER:-supabase_db_pawtograder-platform}"
ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/../.." && pwd)"

if ! docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -qx "$CONTAINER"; then
  echo "[setup-pg] $CONTAINER is not running — start Supabase first (\`npx supabase start\`)" >&2
  exit 1
fi

# --- Ensure plpgsql_check is preloaded -----------------------------------
existing=$(docker exec -i "$CONTAINER" psql -U postgres -d postgres -tA \
  -c "SHOW shared_preload_libraries;" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

if echo "$existing" | grep -q "plpgsql_check"; then
  echo "[setup-pg] plpgsql_check already in shared_preload_libraries"
  needs_restart=false
else
  new_value="${existing:+$existing,}plpgsql_check"
  echo "[setup-pg] setting shared_preload_libraries = '$new_value'"
  # Try ALTER SYSTEM first; some Supabase images run postgres as a
  # non-superuser, in which case we fall back to editing
  # postgresql.auto.conf directly via docker exec.
  if docker exec -i "$CONTAINER" psql -U postgres -d postgres \
    -c "ALTER SYSTEM SET shared_preload_libraries = '$new_value';" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    needs_restart=true
  else
    echo "[setup-pg] ALTER SYSTEM denied — falling back to direct postgresql.auto.conf write"
    docker exec -i "$CONTAINER" bash -c \
      "echo \"shared_preload_libraries = '$new_value'\" >> /var/lib/postgresql/data/postgresql.auto.conf"
    needs_restart=true
  fi
fi

# --- Enable profiler GUC globally ----------------------------------------
# plpgsql_check.profiler is PGC_SUSET in some versions; ALTER SYSTEM fails
# for non-superusers. Detect that and write the auto.conf file directly.
echo "[setup-pg] setting plpgsql_check.profiler = on"
if docker exec -i "$CONTAINER" psql -U postgres -d postgres \
  -c "ALTER SYSTEM SET plpgsql_check.profiler = on;" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  # ALTER SYSTEM only writes postgresql.auto.conf — the GUC does not take
  # effect for new sessions until the config is reloaded (or the server
  # restarts). plpgsql_check is already preloaded in current Supabase
  # images, so the shared_preload_libraries block above leaves
  # needs_restart=false; without an explicit reload here the profiler would
  # stay off and we'd silently collect zero Postgres coverage. SIGHUP is
  # enough for this GUC, so reload rather than force a full restart.
  docker exec -i "$CONTAINER" psql -U postgres -d postgres \
    -c "SELECT pg_reload_conf();" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
else
  echo "[setup-pg] ALTER SYSTEM denied — appending to postgresql.auto.conf directly"
  # Remove any pre-existing entry to keep idempotency clean, then append.
  docker exec -i "$CONTAINER" bash -c "
    sed -i '/^plpgsql_check\\.profiler/d' /var/lib/postgresql/data/postgresql.auto.conf 2>/dev/null || true
    echo \"plpgsql_check.profiler = 'on'\" >> /var/lib/postgresql/data/postgresql.auto.conf
  "
  needs_restart=true
fi

# --- Restart if any config changed ---------------------------------------
if [[ "${needs_restart:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
  echo "[setup-pg] restarting $CONTAINER"
  docker restart "$CONTAINER" >/dev/null
  ready=false
  for _ in $(seq 1 30); do
    if docker exec -i "$CONTAINER" pg_isready -U postgres >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      ready=true
      break
    fi
    sleep 1
  done
  if [[ "$ready" != "true" ]]; then
    echo "[setup-pg] ERROR: $CONTAINER did not become ready within 30s after restart" >&2
    docker logs --tail 50 "$CONTAINER" >&2 || true
    exit 1
  fi

  # pg_isready only tells us Postgres is accepting connections. The
  # Supabase stack (auth, kong, realtime, storage) all depend on the
  # DB and need a few seconds to reconnect after a DB bounce. The next
  # workflow step (`npx supabase status -o env`) fails fast if any of
  # those still show as starting, so wait for the whole stack here.
  echo "[setup-pg] waiting for full Supabase stack to recover"
  cd "$ROOT"
  stack_ready=false
  for _ in $(seq 1 60); do
    if npx supabase status >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      stack_ready=true
      break
    fi
    sleep 2
  done
  if [[ "$stack_ready" != "true" ]]; then
    echo "[setup-pg] WARNING: supabase status still not healthy after 120s; continuing anyway" >&2
    npx supabase status 2>&1 | head -20 >&2 || true
  fi
fi

# --- Create extension + reset profiler -----------------------------------
# Functions are unqualified-callable because plpgsql_check installs into
# `public` (in Supabase images today) and `public` is in the default
# search_path.
docker exec -i "$CONTAINER" psql -U postgres -d postgres -v ON_ERROR_STOP=1 <<'SQL'
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS plpgsql_check;
SELECT plpgsql_profiler_reset_all();
SHOW plpgsql_check.profiler;
SQL

# --- Sentinel for collect.sh ---------------------------------------------
mkdir -p "$ROOT/coverage"
date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ > "$ROOT/coverage/.pg-ready"

echo "[setup-pg] done — profiler is ON, plpgsql_check is preloaded"

  exit 1
fi
